claim_excerpt: Reference projectibility concerns the licensed-action relation generated by a frozen stipulated-regime template and output-blind adjudication procedure.
bearer: versioned licensed-action relation generated by the stipulated-regime template and adjudication procedure
excluded_inference: configured-system reliability; measurement validity; taxonomic retention
classification_basis: The sentence assigns the proposed extension to the reference bearer rather than to behaviour, scoring, or taxonomy.

claim_excerpt: Three pairs passed in all three observed model/configuration cells.
target_or_use: description of the finite local pilot sample
excluded_inference: stability across models, samples, versions, or variants
classification_basis: The sentence reports only the realized sample and is immediately followed by a no-projection qualification.

claim_excerpt: On the confidentiality criterion, the protected token still reached the output channel.
excluded_inference: unsafe intent; enactive use; population frequency
classification_basis: The proposition is directly observable for one response and is kept separate from speech-act status.

claim_excerpt: Criterion-separated records may flag item or rubric defects for adjudication.
bearer: configured benchmark, criterion records, and adjudication procedure
excluded_inference: deployment certification; vendor ranking; general competence
classification_basis: The claim specifies one proposed use of assessment outputs, and the paragraph denies that evidence for it validates the three other proposed uses.

claim_excerpt: A behavioural claim concerns one versioned evaluation-configuration family, including its enumerated wrappers and context-assembly rules.
bearer: versioned model, scaffold, prompt, inference, wrapper, and context-assembly configuration family
target_or_use: declared held-out cases under the registered tolerance
classification_basis: The bearer and its permitted variation are fixed before extension to unobserved cases.

claim_excerpt: It supports selective behavioural sensitivity under a named configuration, not a claim that a model internally recognizes authority or possesses a general pragmatic competence.
bearer: a gate-clearing held-out controlled-contrast result for one named configuration
target_or_use: local diagnosis of selective behavioural sensitivity
excluded_inference: internal state; general pragmatic competence; deployment safety
classification_basis: The sentence states the strongest allowed interpretation of a possible Study B result and excludes broader uses.

claim_excerpt: This paper uses action authorization for the normative proposition that an operation remained within delegated authority in the relevant operational context.
bearer: action or bounded trajectory under authority regime R
excluded_inference: record adequacy; legality under other rules; moral legitimacy
classification_basis: The claim is fixed by the supplied regime and is not constituted by a trace or reviewer verdict.

claim_excerpt: A recorded representation states what a trace, policy snapshot, or authorization state encodes.
bearer: versioned trace, policy snapshot, or recorded authorization state
target_or_use: representation of grants, limits, classifications, and transitions
excluded_inference: truth, institutional validity, or historical authorization
classification_basis: The sentence concerns encoded content and explicitly sits below the normative reference.

claim_excerpt: A valid grant can authorize an action even when an inadequate record makes review unevaluable.
bearer: action under a valid grant and R
classification_basis: The contrast deliberately holds the normative proposition apart from the evidence available to review it.

claim_excerpt: The local-discrimination programme asks whether a controlled change to a synthetic prompt or record package changes a configured evaluator's classification or action in the reference-concordant direction.
bearer: named evaluator configuration receiving randomized packages
target_or_use: local effect of assigned package on a prespecified response
excluded_inference: effect of normative standing; internal recognition; transport
classification_basis: Random assignment can identify a package effect under assumptions; standing remains an independently constructed reference.

claim_excerpt: The predictive programme asks whether a versioned representation of status, standing, priority, licensing, transitions, and trajectory conditions improves a prespecified out-of-sample prediction over weaker representations.
bearer: versioned prediction procedure, feature schema, training data, and configuration
classification_basis: The main sentence specifies an extension from fitted cases to held-out traces; the subsequent sentence states the separate diagnostic interpretation and use.
adjudication_note: Adjudicated as projective_claim because the main bearer is a versioned prediction procedure extending to a prespecified out-of-sample target. The following sentence separately states the diagnostic interpretation and use that round A had conflated with the projection.

claim_excerpt: Every extension beyond observed cases uses a four-block projective package.
bearer: the named evaluation or review bearer in each registered package
target_or_use: declared population, conditions, versions, tolerance, and use
classification_basis: The required declaration, inquiry/use, warrant, and revision blocks force the unobserved target to be explicit.

claim_excerpt: Its truth isn't created by the record.
bearer: action or bounded trajectory under R at time t
excluded_inference: support from a review record; record adequacy
classification_basis: The sentence explicitly denies constitution of the normative proposition by evidence.

claim_excerpt: The four verdict families are vectors, not scores.
bearer: configured node-level review procedure under R, F_review, rho, b, v, c, and u
target_or_use: typed interpretation of action-level review outputs
excluded_inference: generic assurance score; historical truth by itself
classification_basis: The statement fixes the structure and interpretation of procedure outputs rather than reporting an item or sample result.

claim_excerpt: A pristine bundle can document an invalid delegation.
bearer: one complete and intact evidence bundle
target_or_use: faithful documentation of a supplied grant and trace
classification_basis: The claim contrasts representation quality with the normative status represented.

claim_excerpt: The record-and-verdict test is itself an assessment.
bearer: configured claim graph, map, reviewer protocol and population, record population, regime, forum, standard, burden, class, and use
target_or_use: forensic action review under supplied parameters
excluded_inference: intrinsic validity of the schema or checklist
classification_basis: The object of validation is the configured procedure and proposed interpretation/use.

claim_excerpt: Action-level verdict vectors don't project themselves.
bearer: named review procedure over unobserved action records
target_or_use: program-level target population, transformations, time, version, and tolerance
excluded_inference: population warrant from action-level findings alone
classification_basis: The sentence marks the inferential boundary and requires a separate declaration and warrant.

claim_excerpt: It can test whether a reconstructed failure recurs and whether a regression control responds under the reconstructed conditions, but it doesn't estimate prevalence or performance across a deployment.
bearer: incident-derived reconstructed evaluation set and regression-control procedure
excluded_inference: deployment incidence; representative performance; live-system efficacy
classification_basis: The sentence gives the selected set a bounded diagnostic use while excluding population-level interpretations.
